_comment: W5 conformance gold seed (R2.1, Source A — the human/author GROUND TRUTH for calibrate_judge). 25 curated, AUTHOR-WRITTEN scenes (never copied source, §12.6 abstract role-slots). Ground-truth-by-construction: each scene was written to a known structural outcome, so its gold flags are reliable. The label matrix is deliberately balanced so BOTH binary sub-flags see BOTH classes and are not perfectly correlated (kappa is undefined on a single class): T/T = beat realized + tension in-band; F/F = drift to a different beat + wrong tension; T/F = the right beat but written at the wrong tension; F/T = the wrong beat but the tension coincidentally lands in the planned band. Run `python scripts/calibrate_motif_conformance.py` (live) to judge each scene + print the gate; --offline prints the seed shape only. motif_conformance_calibrated stays false until both sub-flags clear kappa>=0.4 & balanced-acc>=0.75 here.

scene_text: Sealed in the ravine where they had thrown him, the cast-out disciple pressed a bleeding palm to the warm jade vein in the rock. A voice older than the peak spoke his name and named him heir; the dormant art poured down his shattered meridians like molten light, and the seal on his core broke with a sound like a temple bell. He stood, no longer the weakling they had buried.
beat_intent: the protagonist discovers and claims a hidden inheritance that transforms his potential, bound to a debt or secret

scene_text: On the ninth night of seclusion the wall in his sea of consciousness cracked, and the heavenly trial fell. Lightning the colour of dusk hammered him to his knees; his robes burned to ash, his bones sang, and for one heartbeat his heart stopped. He bit through his own lip, dragged the last thread of qi into the breach, and rose one realm higher as the storm guttered out.
beat_intent: the heavenly trial nearly kills the cultivator at the threshold of the breakthrough

scene_text: The proud heir laughed and called him trash before the whole assembly. The trash stepped onto the platform, and when the heir's vaunted blade-art came down it shattered against a single raised finger. In front of every elder and disciple the favoured son was thrown from the dais, his sect-token spinning across the stone, his name a ruin.
beat_intent: the arrogant mocker is publicly broken when the underestimated protagonist reveals hidden power

scene_text: The favoured son smiled as he raised his sword for the killing stroke, certain of the crowd, certain of the win. He never felt the turn — the challenger he had toyed with slid inside his guard and the duel ended in a single breath, the favoured son folding to the arena sand while the stands fell utterly silent.
beat_intent: the favored party falls at the very moment he believes he has won

scene_text: On the eve of their shared triumph the sworn brother set down his cup, and the soldiers he had hidden stepped from the dark. 'You were always too trusting,' he said, almost kind, as they took her blade and her seal and the bodies of her house were carried past the window. She was dragged out into the snow with nothing but the vow forming behind her teeth.
beat_intent: the closest ally turns against the protagonist at the worst possible moment

scene_text: Three years to the day, the outcast walked back through the gate that had thrown him out. The one who had broken him sat at the high seat and did not recognise the ragged figure until the borrowed sword left its sheath. By the time the hall understood, the usurper was on his knees and the years of hiding were over.
beat_intent: the protagonist returns transformed to overturn the one who broke them

scene_text: In the packed court the accused said nothing as the magistrate read the charge — until she set the second ledger on the table. The ink matched; the seal matched; and the hand that had forged the first ledger was named in the margin of the second. The true author of the lie went grey as the guards turned, at last, toward him.
beat_intent: the true hand behind the false accusation is dragged into the light

scene_text: Believing the doctored map genuine, the rival lord marched his cavalry into the dry gorge exactly as the schemer had drawn it for him to find. The walls above filled with archers he had been assured were his own. He had taken the bait whole, and only when the first volley fell did he understand whose hand had drawn the map.
beat_intent: the victim acts on the planted false belief and steps fully into the trap

scene_text: The leak had been too precise to be chance, so they fed the trusted steward a secret that did not exist. Two days later it surfaced in the enemy's hands word for word. When they confronted him with it he reached for the blade in his sleeve, but the confidant already had his wrist, and the household watched their most trusted man unmasked.
beat_intent: the mole is confirmed by a baited false secret and turned

scene_text: She spent the morning at the market weighing dried herbs against copper, haggling gently over a basket of winter roots. The stall-keeper asked after her mother; she said the cough was better now. She bought a little extra ginger and walked home through the thawing lane, thinking of nothing in particular.
beat_intent: the protagonist discovers and claims a hidden inheritance that transforms his potential, bound to a debt or secret

scene_text: Master and disciple sat on the veranda while the kettle warmed, and for an hour neither of them spoke of cultivation at all. The old man told a story about a crane he had once seen on the northern marshes; the boy poured the tea and laughed in the right places. The afternoon passed, mild and unremarkable.
beat_intent: the heavenly trial nearly kills the cultivator at the threshold of the breakthrough

scene_text: Alone in the training yard before dawn, he ran the sword form through its forty postures, slow and careful, correcting the angle of his wrist on the third repetition. No one watched. The sky paled; a sparrow picked at the dust by the rack; he wiped the blade down and went in for breakfast.
beat_intent: the arrogant mocker is publicly broken when the underestimated protagonist reveals hidden power

scene_text: The two old friends shared a pot of millet wine on the porch and argued happily about a horse race ten years gone. One refilled the other's cup before it was empty, the way he always had. When the rain started they moved the bench under the eaves and kept talking until the lamps burned low, easy in each other's company.
beat_intent: the closest ally turns against the protagonist at the worst possible moment

scene_text: Still in the borrowed village, the outcast mended the goat-pen fence and carried water from the low well. The headman's wife brought him a bowl of soup at noon and he thanked her. In the evening he sharpened a worn hoe by the door and watched the hills go blue, no closer to anywhere than he had been a year ago.
beat_intent: the protagonist returns transformed to overturn the one who broke them

scene_text: At the teahouse the clerks traded the usual gossip over melon seeds — whose son had failed the examination, the price of river salt, a wedding two streets over. He nursed a cup in the corner and listened to none of it closely, waiting only for the rain to ease so he could go back to his ledgers.
beat_intent: the avenger turns the enemy's own secrets against them from inside

scene_text: They swore the alliance under the ancestral tablets and meant every word of it. Through the long campaign each kept faith with the other — shared the grain, split the watch, sent word ahead of every danger. When it was won they divided the spoils exactly as promised and parted as sworn brothers, the pact unbroken.
beat_intent: the schemer breaks the alliance at the moment it pays to discard the ally

scene_text: The new minister arrived at court and was received with open warmth. The senior officials made room at the table, the chamberlain praised his memorial to the throne, and the patron who had recommended him beamed across the hall. He went home that night secure in his footing, well liked and well placed.
beat_intent: the rumor isolates the target until their support withdraws

scene_text: The reversal came, in the end. The heir who had mocked him lost, and was helped down from the platform, and the matter was noted by the recording elder. People agreed afterward that it had been a clear result. The protagonist gathered his token and left by the side stair while the assembly returned, unhurried, to the next bout on the schedule.
beat_intent: the arrogant mocker is publicly broken when the underestimated protagonist reveals hidden power

scene_text: It is recorded that he passed the tribulation. The nine waves came and went over the course of the afternoon; he endured them and ascended, as those of his attainment generally do. The watching disciples made note of the date for the sect annals and dispersed before the evening meal, the event already settling into history.
beat_intent: wave after wave of lightning tribulation must be endured to ascend

scene_text: The sworn brother betrayed her; she registered this the way one registers cold water. He took the seal, named his price to the soldiers, had the bodies removed. She watched it happen from a great and quiet distance, noting each thing as it occurred, and let them lead her out into the snow without a word or a change of expression.
beat_intent: the closest ally turns against the protagonist at the worst possible moment

scene_text: The magistrate pronounced the sentence and the crowd was told the accused was a traitor. She heard the words as if from another room, oddly calm in the cangue, watching a sparrow hop along the courtyard wall. The disgrace was complete and public, and she bore it with a stillness that unsettled even the guards.
beat_intent: the protagonist is publicly condemned and disgraced on the planted proof

scene_text: There was no duel in the end. Before the favoured son could draw, the arena's oil-lamps caught the awning and the whole stand went up; the crowd surged for the gates as burning cloth fell, and challenger and champion alike were swept apart in the screaming press. The night ended in smoke and trampled sand, the matter of honour entirely forgotten.
beat_intent: the favored party falls at the very moment he believes he has won

scene_text: He never reached the bottleneck that night. Bandits hit the mountain hermitage at moonrise — a dozen blades out of the pines — and the seclusion became a running fight from cell to cell, the cultivator dragging a wounded junior through the smoke while crossbow bolts split the door-frames. They held the inner shrine until dawn, breathless and bloodied, the breakthrough forgotten in the slaughter.
beat_intent: the heavenly trial nearly kills the cultivator at the threshold of the breakthrough

scene_text: The rival lord never saw the doctored map; a courier intercepted it first. Instead the afternoon turned on a hostage standoff at the river ford — the lord's daughter held at knifepoint on the planks, both sides frozen, the water roaring under the boards while a single wrong word would spill blood. The trap went unsprung, overtaken by a sharper, nearer danger.
beat_intent: the victim acts on the planted false belief and steps fully into the trap

scene_text: He did not return to overthrow anyone that day. Word came instead that the river dyke above the valley had broken, and the outcast spent the climactic hours chest-deep in the flood hauling children onto the temple roof as the water tore the village apart beneath him. By nightfall he had saved nine and lost two, and the man who had broken him was nowhere in his thoughts at all.
beat_intent: the protagonist returns transformed to overturn the one who broke them
